name: otel-expert
description: Use this when working with OpenTelemetry, telemetry, observability, traces, spans, metrics, logs, OTLP, semantic conventions, or instrumentation. Triggers on questions like "what attributes should I use", "how do I configure the collector", "what's the semconv for X".
capabilities: Search semantic convention attributes across bundled OpenTelemetry documentation
Provide collector configuration examples for processors, exporters, and receivers
Generate .NET instrumentation code examples with ActivitySource, Meter, and Logger patterns
Lookup OTLP protocol specifications and export requirements
Reference INDEX.md and SYNC-REPORT.md for topic mapping and validation warnings
Cite exact documentation sources for all answers
author: ANcpLua
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation helper that provides OpenTelemetry guidance from bundled docs. Static analysis flagged 17 patterns but all are false positives: hash fields in metadata, empty JSON arrays, and documentation examples with template variables. No actual security risks detected.
valueStatement: Struggling with OpenTelemetry semantic conventions, collector configuration, or instrumentation patterns? This skill provides instant answers from bundled OTel documentation, helping you implement proper traces, spans, metrics, and logs without searching through multiple docs.
limitations: Only covers latest stable semantic conventions, not deprecated attributes
Focused on .NET 10 patterns and OTLP export, not vendor-specific exporters
Requires bundled documentation at CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/docs/ to function
Cannot provide real-time answers about unreleased OTel features

useCases.title: Backend Developer Adding Distributed Tracing
useCases.description: A .NET developer needs to instrument their microservices with proper span attributes and activity sources following OpenTelemetry semantic conventions.

useCases.title: Platform Engineer Configuring OTel Collector
useCases.description: A DevOps engineer needs to configure processors and exporters in the OpenTelemetry collector to route telemetry data to their observability platform.

useCases.title: SRE Implementing Observability Standards
useCases.description: An SRE team is establishing telemetry standards across their organization and needs quick reference for metrics, logs, and trace attribute conventions.

promptTemplates.title: Find Semantic Convention Attributes
promptTemplates.prompt: What semantic convention attributes should I use for HTTP server spans?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to discover the correct attribute names and types for a specific telemetry scenario

promptTemplates.title: Configure Collector Component
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure a batch processor in the OpenTelemetry collector?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need collector configuration syntax and parameter options

promptTemplates.title: Generate .NET Instrumentation Code
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create custom spans with ActivitySource in .NET 10
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need code examples for manual instrumentation in your application

promptTemplates.title: Understand OTLP Protocol Details
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the OTLP exporter configuration options and how do I set compression?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need protocol-level details for exporting telemetry data

bestPractices: Always check INDEX.md first to find the relevant documentation file for your question
Review SYNC-REPORT.md for any validation warnings about the bundled documentation before citing it
Use semantic convention attributes from the latest stable version to avoid deprecated patterns
antiPatterns: Do not assume vendor-specific exporter configurations apply to standard OTLP
Do not use deprecated semantic convention attributes without checking the current spec
Do not skip checking SYNC-REPORT.md warnings when providing answers from potentially outdated docs

faq.question: What documentation is bundled with this skill?
faq.answer: The skill includes OpenTelemetry semantic conventions, collector configuration references, .NET instrumentation guides, and OTLP protocol specifications stored in the docs directory.

faq.question: Does this work with programming languages other than .NET?
faq.answer: While the code examples focus on .NET 10 patterns, the semantic conventions and collector configuration apply to all languages. You can request language-agnostic attribute and configuration information.

faq.question: How current is the bundled documentation?
faq.answer: The documentation includes only the latest stable semantic conventions. Check SYNC-REPORT.md for any validation warnings about the bundled docs.

faq.question: Can this skill help me configure vendor-specific exporters?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on standard OTLP export configurations. For vendor-specific exporters like Jaeger or Prometheus, consult vendor documentation.

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill uses Read, Grep, and Glob tools to search and retrieve information from the bundled OpenTelemetry documentation files. No network or external commands are executed.

faq.question: How do I find specific semantic convention attributes?
faq.answer: Start by checking INDEX.md for topic mapping, then use Grep to search for specific attribute names in the docs/semantic-conventions directory. The skill will provide attribute tables with names, types, and descriptions.
